title: Executive Order 12340—Amendments to the Manual for Courts-Martial, United States, 1969 (Revised Edition)

flags.justification: Narrow, specific procedural amendments to an existing military-justice code; no new broad authority is created.
Extremely detailed procedural rules throughout, including exact deadlines and exact categories of counsel availability.
Multiple distinct procedural amendments all coherently serve one system: court-martial procedure under the Uniform Code of Military Justice.
Extensive specificity throughout; even the more discretionary determinations (individual-counsel availability) have a defined appeal chain and, per paragraph 48b(4), judicial review of a denial.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
Standing-procedural-framework category strongly applies: this is one of seven listed amending orders cited in its own preamble, evidencing extensive periodic revision.

Sec. 13 requires the Secretary of Defense to transmit a copy of this order to Congress under 10 U.S.C. §836 — a genuine, explicit congressional-notification requirement built into the amendment process.
Extensive appeal and judicial-review chains (Court of Military Review, Court of Military Appeals) provide real external checks.
A routine, technical procedural amendment within an already-comprehensive system; no novel authority claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/16 = 0.0%. A dense but extremely well-specified procedural order, notable for its explicit, mandatory congressional-transmission requirement.
